translations.![Key-Chain UUID欄位有值時取值，無則取IDFA的UUID值並回寫: ![Retrieve UUID from Key-Chain if present; otherwise, get UUID from IDFA and write it back](/assets/a4bc3bce7513/1*-8rufG1QW-J5tn6ZadT17A.jpeg)
translations.**唯需注意：當所有同Vendor的APP都移除後再重裝就會產生新的UUID (:
**Note: When all apps from the same vendor are deleted and then reinstalled, a new UUID will be generated (** if both com.518.work and com.518.job are deleted, then reinstalling com.518.work will generate a new UUID **)**  
**Similarly, if you have only one app, deleting and reinstalling it will generate a new UUID**

translations.Key-Chain UUID欄位有值時取值，無則取IDFA的UUID值並回寫\n: Retrieve the value from the Key-Chain UUID field if it exists; otherwise, get the IDFA UUID value and write it back.
translations.Lighting Talk的部分在台下聽得也好想上去分享😂 下次要提早做好準備了: I really wanted to go up and share during the Lighting Talk session while listening from the audience 😂 Next time, I’ll prepare earlier!
translations.[**ID** entifier **F** or **V** endor](h:
[**ID** entifier **F** or **V** endor](https://www.jianshu.com/p/b810d7e007ad){:target="_blank"} (IDFV): The current mainstream solution 🏆  
The concept is that Apple generates a UUID for the user based on your Bundle ID prefix. The same Bundle ID prefix will generate the same UUID. For example: com.518.work/com.518.job on the same device will get the same UUID.  
As the original term "ID For Vendor" implies, Apple considers apps with the same prefix as from the same vendor, so sharing the UUID is allowed.
translations.[Finger Printing (Canvas/User-Agent…)](h:
[Finger Printing (Canvas/User-Agent…)](https://medium.com/@ravielakshmanan/web-browser-uniqueness-and-fingerprinting-7eac3c381805){:target="_blank"}: Not researched, but this is mainly used to generate the same UUID for Safari and apps, used in [Deferred Deep Linking](https://www.jianshu.com/p/fa48387d56ea){:target="_blank"}  
[AmIUnique?](https://amiunique.org/){:target="_blank"}
translations.```swift\nif let data = DEVICE_UUID.data:
```swift
if let data = DEVICE_UUID.data(using: .utf8) {
    let query = [
        kSecClass as String       : kSecClassGenericPassword as String,
        kSecAttrAccount as String : "DEVICE_UUID",
        kSecValueData as String   : data ] as [String : Any]
    
    SecItemDelete(query as CFDictionary) // Delete existing item if any
    SecItemAdd(query as CFDictionary, nil) // Add new item to Keychain
}
```
translations.iPlayground 2018 回來 & UUID那些事\n\n: iPlayground 2018 Recap & The Story of UUID
translations.上週六、日跑去參加 [iPlayground](https://iplaygro: Last Saturday and Sunday, I attended the [iPlayground](https://iplayground.io/){:target="_blank"} Apple software developer conference. A colleague passed the event information to me, and I wasn't familiar with it before going.
translations.但這其中也波及到很多正當守法的用戶，像是本來用UUID當使用者的資料解密KEY或: However, this also affected many legitimate users. For example, those who originally used UUID as the user's data decryption key or for device identification were greatly impacted. I really admire the engineers from that time—they had to come up with alternative solutions quickly while dealing with complaints from both bosses and users.
translations.兩天下來，整題活動跟時程安排流暢，議程內容：\n\n: Over the two days, the entire event and schedule flowed smoothly. The agenda included:
translations.凋零的Dirty Code；聽得心有戚戚，在心裡會心一笑；技術債就是這樣一直累積: Withering Dirty Code; it resonates deeply and brings a knowing smile inside. Technical debt accumulates this way—rushed development leads to quick but poorly structured solutions, and successors lack time to refactor, so the debt keeps growing. In the end, the only option might be to start over from scratch.
translations.去參加研討會的目的不外乎就是要： **增加廣度** ，吸收新知、了解生態、碰一些: The purpose of attending a seminar is basically to: **broaden your horizons** by absorbing new knowledge, understanding the ecosystem, and encountering topics you don't usually deal with; and **deepen your understanding** by reviewing familiar topics to see if you missed anything or if there are alternative approaches you haven't discovered.
translations.因為我在其他Extension Target也需要參照所以直接包成一個閉包參數使: Because I also need to reference it in other Extension Targets, I wrapped it directly as a closure parameter for use.
translations.因為我聽完回去後馬上實際應用到APP上；這堂是由Zonble前輩主講，我聽到從i: Because I immediately applied what I learned to my app after the session; this class was taught by senior developer Zonble, and I was amazed hearing about development from iPhone OS 2 up to iOS 12. Since I started late in the industry, I began coding from iOS 11/Swift 4, so I never experienced the chaotic period caused by Apple's API changes.
translations.因為這個特性，我們公司的其他APP是使用Key-Chain來解決這個問題，聽了講: Because of this feature, other apps in our company use the Keychain to solve this problem. After listening to the senior speaker's advice, we confirmed that this approach is correct!
translations.如果嫌 Key-Chain 操作太繁瑣可以自行封裝或使用第三方套件。\n\n: If you find Keychain operations too cumbersome, you can encapsulate them yourself or use third-party libraries.
translations.實用的：測試類 (XCUITest、依賴注入)、SpriteKit 做動畫效果的: Practical: Testing classes (XCUITest, dependency injection), alternatives to SpriteKit for animation effects, GraphQL
translations.想想UUID從可以取得到封鎖也是蠻合理的；如果是用在良善的地方：辨識使用者裝置、: It makes sense that UUID access was eventually restricted; when used properly, it helps identify user devices, supports advertising, or allows third parties to use uniqueness for ad targeting. However, if a company has malicious intent, they could reverse-engineer this mechanism to learn about the phone owner. For example, having travel apps + Taipei bus apps + BMW app + baby care apps could suggest that you often travel abroad, have children, and live in Taipei. Combined with personal data you enter in apps, the potential misuse is unimaginable.
translations.我才知道原來這是第一屆，真的有榮幸能夠參加，所有工作人員跟講者辛苦了！\n\n: I just found out this is the first edition. I'm truly honored to participate. Thanks to all the staff and speakers for their hard work!
translations.抄了許多筆記可以回來慢慢研究回味。\n\n: I took many notes to review and study at my own pace later.
translations.會後的offical party，酒水食物場地都很有誠意，聽前輩們的真心話吐露，: The official party after the event had generous drinks, food, and venue. Listening to the seniors' honest stories was not only relaxing and fun but also helped me gain a lot of workplace soft skills.
translations.有任何問題及指教歡迎 [與我聯絡](https://www.zhgchg.li/: If you have any questions or feedback, feel free to [contact me](https://www.zhgchg.li/contact){:target="_blank"} .
translations.本篇文章以取得UUID辨識裝置唯一值為主，如果是要找知道使用者裝了哪些APP的替: This article focuses on obtaining a UUID to uniquely identify a device. If you are looking for alternatives to know which apps a user has installed, you can refer to the following keywords for search methods: [UIPasteboard pasteboardWithName: create: (using clipboard to share between apps)](https://link.medium.com/YTheNPnHH7){:target="_blank"}, canOpenURL: info.plist LSApplicationQueriesSchemes (using canOpenURL to check if an app is installed, must be listed in info.plist, up to 50 entries).
translations.測試類(Design Patterns in XCUITest) [KKBOX的: Test Classes (Design Patterns in XCUITest) [KKBOX Seniors](https://www.facebook.com/TestingWithKK/){:target="_blank"} openly shared their methods, code examples, encountered issues, and solutions without holding back. This session was one of the most helpful for our work. Testing is an area I've always wanted to improve, so I plan to study it thoroughly.
translations.用MAC Address當UUID，但後來也被BAN了\n: Using MAC Address as UUID, but Later It Was Also Banned
translations.真功夫：深入拆解Swift、iOS 越獄/Tweak開發、Redux\n: Real Skills: In-Depth Analysis of Swift, iOS Jailbreak/Tweak Development, Redux
translations.腳踏車Project 印象深刻，用iPhone手機當感測器感測腳踏車踏板轉動，直: The bicycle project was impressive. It used an iPhone as a sensor to detect pedal rotations, allowing the user to switch slides by riding the bike on stage. (The senior developer's main goal was to create an open-source version of Zwift and shared many pitfalls, such as client/server communication, latency issues, and magnetic interference.)
translations.趣味的：腳踏車、凋零的Code、iOS/API 演進史、威利在哪裡(CoreML: Fun topics: Bicycles, Decaying Code, iOS/API Evolution, Where's Wally (CoreML Vision)
